title: Non-record: SwiGLU + EMA + TTT Memorization Analysis (val_bpb=1.1679)
body:
## Summary

**Verified val_bpb = 1.1679** (standard sliding-window eval, no TTT, 8xH100 SXM, 4238 steps at 141ms/step).

This is a non-record submission. The primary contribution is a **TTT memorization diagnostic** that distinguishes genuine domain adaptation from test-set memorization in multi-epoch TTT submissions.

## Verified Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Verified BPB (no TTT, standard sliding eval)** | **1.1679** |
| Training steps | 4,238 (8xH100 SXM, ~141ms/step) |
| Artifact size | 15.53 MB |

## Research Contribution: TTT Memorization Diagnostic

Per Issue #402, multi-epoch TTT is not valid for record claims. We ran extensive multi-epoch experiments to develop a diagnostic for the competition community.

**Method**: After multi-epoch TTT adaptation, run standard sliding-window eval (stride=64) on the adapted weights. Compare this "sliding diagnostic" to the TTT-loop BPB:

| TTT Config | TTT-Loop BPB | Sliding Diagnostic | Gap | Interpretation |
|------------|-------------|-------------------|-----|----------------|
| 0 epochs (baseline) | — | 1.1679 | — | No adaptation |
| 3 epochs, flat 5e-4 | 1.1032 | 1.0476 | -0.056 | Sliding BETTER — real adaptation |
| 5 epochs, cosine 7e-4 | 1.0101 | 1.0022 | -0.008 | Sliding BETTER — real adaptation |
| **10 epochs, flat 5e-4** | **0.8566** | **0.9229** | **+0.066** | **TTT-loop better — MEMORIZATION** |

**Key finding**: At 10 epochs, the TTT-loop reports 0.8566 BPB, but the sliding diagnostic reveals actual prediction quality is only 0.9229 BPB. The 0.066 gap is pure memorization. **Submissions reporting sub-0.95 BPB from high-epoch TTT should be examined with this diagnostic.**

We also found that **global cosine LR decay** across epochs delays the memorization boundary (5 epochs with cosine stays clean, while 5 epochs with flat LR begins to memorize), and that **per-layer LR multipliers** (later layers adapt faster) improve adaptation quality.

## Architecture

SwiGLU MLP (mult=2.0), EMA (0.9985), int5 quantization (all weights), 5% magnitude pruning, zstd-22. 10 layers, 512-dim, 8 heads, 4 KV heads, BigramHash, SmearGate. Built on thwu1 PR #180 / unnir PR #162.

## EBLS Findings

Empirical Bayes Layer Sharing with learned shrinkage gammas:
- MLP gammas → 0.0000 (fully shared MLP is optimal under compression)
- Attention gammas near-zero except trace specialization in early layers
- See companion repo: [parameter-golf-ebls](https://github.com/Robby955/parameter-golf-ebls)

## Negative Results

- **Trigram hashing**: 3-token XOR hash worse than bigram (1.0532 vs 1.0320)
- **Late QAT**: 13ms/step overhead costs training steps, net negative
- **11 layers**: Exceeds 16MB (SWIGLU 2.0) or too slow (SWIGLU 1.7)
- **Per-epoch cosine**: Resetting cosine each epoch worse than flat LR

## Credits

thwu1 PR #180, unnir PR #162, felipe-parodi (EMA), sjp611 (TTT concept), JoeProAI PR #462 (sequential TTT, SwiGLU), PR #509/#508 (epoch scaling, embedding freeze), ndokutovich PR #486 (per-layer LR, global cosine TTT).
